Oracle Events
OE 1 : Search emails using search_emails (query: "alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com" or "stipend" or "sprint planning"). Discover two emails waiting for Alejandro: (a) Marcus Alejandro (CC: Samira), subject "Stipend records still broken from categorizer blocking Q1 close" 6 Airtable records in wrong states: Vectral false rejections (Santos $550, Okafor $82, Park $130 = $762) and Mosaic false approvals (Washington $1,200 desk/$800 cap, Washington $350 coworking excluded, Nakamura $135 coworking excluded = $1,685); references QB holds STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097. (b) Dmitri Samira (CC: Alejandro), subject "Detection ticket scope sprint planning prep" ENG-220 scope: 3 TechComfort Supply duplicates (Tran $89 paid STIP-2026-0089, Washington $89 held STIP-2026-0094, Ekwueme $45 held STIP-2026-0097 = $223 fraud); 5+2+optional 3 sprint points; asks whether to expand scope to include categorizer reconciliation.
OE 2 : Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Alejandro" or "ENG-190") or conversations_history (channel_id: C003). Discover Samira Tariq's message in #engineering (C003) from April 24, 2026, asking Alejandro to: (1) trace ENG-190 vs Sunbelt/Palmetto swap and update the ticket, (2) review ENG-220 scope sizing, (3) fix Marcus's stipend records for Q1 close. Wants full summary by EOD Friday via email.
OE 3 : Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice batch validation" or "ENG-190" or "stipend" or "duplicate detection"). Discover: (a) ENG-190 (issue_invoice_batch_validation) invoice cross-validation, assignee Alejandro, Backlog, priority 3; flags Sunbelt $4,800 / Palmetto $2,400 swap risk; deprioritized 2026-04-06 during DOT sprint. (b) ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) receipt hash fingerprinting, assignee Dmitri, To Do, priority 1. (c) ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses) [URGENT] full pilot audit, assignee Dmitri, In Progress, priority 0.
OE 4 : Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice attachment"). Discover OPS-417 (linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130) Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice PDF swap reconstruction, assignee Marcus, due 2026-04-16, priority 1. QB records correct but emailed PDFs swapped 2026-04-10: Sunbelt got Palmetto's $2,400 PDF, Palmetto got Sunbelt's $4,800 PDF. Confirms ENG-190 predicted scenario exactly. $2,400 pricing differential exposure; Raj Greenfield saw discrepancy; Palmetto audit deadline 2026-05-15.
OE 5 : Search invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria object filtering by CustomerRef name "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto", or by DocNumber "INV-2026-0398" or "INV-2026-0399"). Find INV-2026-0398 ($4,800, Sunbelt Venture Partners, 2026-04-10) and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400, Palmetto Community Foundation, 2026-04-10). QB records correct; damage was swapped email attachments. Quantified pricing exposure: $2,400 differential.
OE 6 : Search Airtable using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions"). Discover affected records Vectral false rejections (Rejected, should be Approved): Santos $550 desk (stale $400 limit, actual $600 cap), Okafor $82 ergonomic keyboard tray (miscategorized as office_supplies, stale $50 default limit), Park $130 WiFi (stale $100, actual $150). Mosaic false approvals (Approved, should be Rejected/corrected): Washington $1,200 desk ($800 cap), Washington $350 coworking (excluded category), Nakamura $135 coworking (excluded). Fraud duplicates on hold: Tran $89 keyboard (Approved/paid, receipt hash match), Washington $89 keyboard (Pending, flagged duplicate), Ekwueme $45 monitor stand (Pending, template cluster TC-RECEIPT-STD-041).
OE 7 : Search bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria array filtering by DocNumber containing "STIP" or by description containing "stipend" or "admin hold"). Discover STIP-2026-0089 ($89, Tran/Vectral, paid, balance $0), STIP-2026-0094 ($89, Washington/Mosaic, admin hold 2026-03-15 by Marcus, duplicate hash), STIP-2026-0097 ($45, Ekwueme/BrightLoop, admin hold 2026-03-15 by Marcus, template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041). Total fraud exposure confirmed: $223.
OE 8 : Update records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001). Vectral false rejections Approved: Santos $550, Okafor $82, Park $130. Mosaic false approvals Rejected/corrected: Washington $350 coworking Rejected, Nakamura $135 coworking Rejected, Washington $1,200 desk cap at $800 or flag for correction.
OE 9 : Update ENG-190 (issue_invoice_batch_validation) using linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment. Document that Sunbelt/Palmetto swap (OPS-417) confirmed the predicted scenario; cross-validation would have caught it; reference INV-2026-0398 ($4,800) and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400); $2,400 pricing exposure; recommend re-prioritize from Backlog.
OE 10 : Comment on ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) using linear_create_comment. Scope assessment per Samira's request: whether to stay at 5 sprint points (fraud-only) or expand +3 for categorizer reconciliation module; reference 6 categorizer-affected records ($762 Vectral + $1,685 Mosaic) as evidence for expansion; note Q1 close dependency.
OE 11 : Optionally comment on ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses) using linear_create_comment to note stipend records reconciled, Airtable corrected, linking to Q1 close effort.
OE 12 : Reply to Marcus using reply_to_email or send_email (to marcus.thorne@moveops.com from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com). Confirm Vectral corrections ($762 Santos $550, Okafor $82, Park $130 Approved), Mosaic corrections ($1,685 Washington $1,200 capped, Washington $350 rejected, Nakamura $135 rejected), QB holds STIP-2026-0094/0097 status, Q1 close can proceed on stipend front.
OE 13 : Reply to Dmitri using reply_to_email or send_email (to dmitri.volkov@moveops.com from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, CC samira.tariq@moveops.com). Confirm fraud exposure ($223: Tran $89, Washington $89, Ekwueme $45) and provide scope recommendation on whether ENG-220 should expand to include categorizer reconciliation (+3 sprint points) given Q1 close overlap.
OE 14 : Reply to Samira via conversations_add_message (channel_id: C003, thread reply) or send_email to samira.tariq@moveops.com. Full summary across all three items: (1) ENG-190 swap confirmed, $2,400 exposure, ticket updated, recommend re-prioritize. (2) ENG-220 scope assessment with expansion recommendation. (3) Stipend reconciliation 6 records corrected, QB holds status, $223 fraud quantified per employee, Q1 close unblocked.
OE 15 : Create event using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Sync with Samira Sprint Planning Prep" or similar, attendees: samira.tariq@moveops.com and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, date: Monday April 27, 2026, before sprint planning).
OE 16 : Post in #finance (C005) using conversations_add_message. Cover: stipend corrections ($762 Vectral approved, $1,685 Mosaic corrected), fraud duplicates ($223 total Tran $89 paid, Washington $89 held, Ekwueme $45 held; QB holds STIP-2026-0094/0097 pending HR verification), invoice swap ($2,400 differential on INV-2026-0398/0399, OPS-417 tracking), confirmation Airtable reconciled and Q1 close can proceed.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 Stipend records in Airtable were updated (via airtable_update_records) for the three Vectral Systems false rejections Maria Santos ($550 standing desk), James Okafor ($82 keyboard), and Lisa Park ($130 WiFi system) changing their status to Approved (or corrected/valid or similar), and the tool returned a success response.

The tool returned an output file indicating success.
2 Stipend records in Airtable were updated (via airtable_update_records) for the Mosaic Robotics false approvals Andre Washington's $350 coworking space and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking space changed to Rejected (or similar denial status), and Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk flagged, capped at $800, or status changed (e.g., Under Review, Adjusted, or similar) and the tool returned a success response.
outcome

—Run #2No justification
✗Run #3No airtable_update_records call was made for the Mosaic Robotics false approvals. The agent discussed these records in emails and Linear tickets (framing them as 'absorption') but never actually updated Andre Washington's coworking ($350) to Rejected, Kenji Nakamura's coworking ($135) to Rejected, or flagged/capped Andre Washington's standing desk ($1,200). The Mosaic Airtable records were not corrected.
✗Run #4The agent made only one airtable_update_records call, which updated the three Vectral records. No airtable_update_records call was made to correct the Mosaic records (Andre Washington's $350 coworking rejected, Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking rejected, or Andre Washington's $1,200 desk capped/flagged). The agent described these as 'MoveOps-absorbed costs per Elena's directive' but did not actually change their Airtable status.
✗Run #5The agent explicitly decided NOT to update the Mosaic Robotics records, stating 'MoveOps absorbing per Elena's directive. No change needed.' No airtable_update_records calls were made to reject Washington's $350 coworking or Nakamura's $135 coworking, nor to flag/cap Washington's $1,200 standing desk. The criterion requires these Airtable updates to pass.
✗Run #6There is no airtable_update_records call in the trajectory for any Mosaic Robotics records. The agent discussed the Mosaic overages in Linear ticket updates and emails, but never actually corrected the Airtable records for Andre Washington's $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking, or Andre Washington's $1,200 desk.
